rulings.Case Title: TALIA KENNEDY VS. META PLATFORMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatoriesemployment No. 216.1 Request For Monetary Sanctions, Against Defendant Meta Platforms, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2025, line 1, 1 - PLAINTIFF TALIA KENNEDY's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatoriesemployment No. 216.1 Request For Monetary Sanctions, Against Defendant Meta Platforms, Inc; Plaintiff Talia Kennedy's motion to compel further responses to interrogatory number 216 is denied and defendant Meta Platoform's request for sanctions is also denied. Looking at the big picture of this case, while Meta Platform's supplemental response to interrogatory number 216 clearly could have been fuller, Ms. Kennedy is not prejudiced by the response and requiring Meta Platforms to further supplement its response would be tantamount to "busy work." Since Ms. Kennedy's position has substantial justification, monetary sanctions are denied. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TALIA KENNEDY VS. META PLATFORMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Documents In Their Original, Native, And Unaltered Form, And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $7,560.00 Against Defendant Meta Platforms, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2025, line 1, 2 - PLAINTIFF TALIA KENNEDY's Motion To Compel Documents In Their Original, Native, And Unaltered Form, And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $7,560.00 Against Defendant Meta Platforms, Inc.; Plaintiff Talia Kennedy's motion to compel documents in their original, native, and unaltered form and request for monetary sanctions is granted as to the request to compel production and denied and to the request for monetary sanctions. Ms. Kennedy has shown good cause for her request to compel documents in their original, native, and unaltered form and defendants fail to provide evidentiary support for their contention that doing so imposes an undue burden on them. However, since defendants' position has substantial justification, monetary sanctions are denied. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: MATHILDE FROUSTEY VS. SAN FRANCISCO BALLET ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions Against Platintiff And Plaintiff'S Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2025, line 2, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BALLET ASSOCIATION's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions Against Platintiff And Plaintiff'S Counsel; Defendant San Francisco Ballet Association's motion to compel further responses to requests for admission and for monetary sanctions is denied in its entirety. The sanctions requests by both sides are denied. The Ballet Association's effort to require plaintiff to provide substantive responses in accordance with its understanding of the pertinent facts is understandable, but is not supported by applicable. If Ms. Froustey has been disingenuous in her responses, the Code of Civil Procedure provides appropriate remedies, including cost of proof sanctions. Neither side's positions lacked substantial justification, precluding the award of monetary sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: MARY GEAN ET AL VS. EDMUND GEAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories, For Production Of Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2025, line 3, PLAINTIFFS' Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories, For Production Of Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions; Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to Plaintiff Tedmund Gean's Form Interrogatories - General, Set One (interrogatories 9.2 and 15.1), Plaintiff Tedmund Gean's First Set of Special Interrogatories (interrogatories 5, 7, 13, 15, and 29), and Plaintiff Tedmund Gean's Demand For Production of Documents, Set One (Categories 1-49) is granted. Defendant missed the deadline to respond to this discovery and therefore, waived all objections to the discovery. (Busch Decl., pars. 4-15; CCP 2030.290, 2031.300.) Defendant's supplemental responses, which continue to assert objections, are therefore deficient. Defendant's responses to the production request also failed to comply with CCP 2031.230 ["A representation of inability to comply with the particular demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling shall affirm that a diligent search and a reasonable inquiry has been made in an effort to comply with that demand. This statement shall also specify whether the inability to comply is because the particular item or category has never existed, has been destroyed, has been lost, misplaced, or stolen, or has never been, or is no longer, in the possession, custody, or control of the responding party. The statement shall set forth the name and address of any natural person or organization known or believed by that party to have possession, custody, or control of that item or category of item."] Defendant shall provide objection-free responses that comply with CCP 2031.230 (if applicable) by January 31, 2025. Defendant shall pay sanctions of $3,000 by February 14, 2025. Plaintiff also properly characterizes the August 14, 2024, order and defendant shall pay the remaining amount of those sanctions by February 14, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BREDA MITCHELL VS. MARTINA MITCHELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2025, line 5, 1 - DEFENDANT MARTINA MITCHELL MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT Defendant Martina Mitchell's motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint is denied as to all portions sought to be stricken except the prayer for reformation. Other than the prayer for reformation, none of the portions of the complaint sought to be stricken are legally invalid. Because the complaint fails to allege ultimate facts supporting reformation of any contract, the prayer for reformation is stricken. Plaintiff Breda Mitchell is granted leave to file an amended complaint no later than February 6, 2025 to allege, if she can do so in good faith, factual allegations supporting a prayer for reformation of a written contract. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TOMI MENDEZ ET AL VS. CELTIC BANK CORPORATION, A UTAH CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Corrected Notice And Demurrer To Defndants' First Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2025, line 6, 1 - PLAINTIFFS TOMI MENDEZ, TYNIA BAISDEN, FANTASIA HILLIARD, BRINDA MOORE, CECILIA SMITH'S Demurrer To Defendants' First Amended Answer Plaintiffs' demurrer to the first amended answer is overruled in its entirety. The amended answer sufficiently alleges all eight affirmative defenses. If plaintiffs need more information about those defenses, they may propound appropriate discovery. If they believe the affirmative defenses do not apply or lack an evidentiary basis, they may move for summary adjudication, file a motion in limine and/or raise the issue with the trial judge during a jury instructions conference. At this early stage of the case, in keeping with litigation norms in the Bay Area, defendants are not required to do more to allege their affirmative defenses. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: KARTINA WAUER VS. HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Arbitration Binding Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2025, line 8, DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA'S Motion To Compel Arbitration Binding Arbitration *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL POWELL VS. SAN FRANCISCO FIRE DEPARTMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2025, line 7, 1 - DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the complaint is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff Daniel Powell is given leave to file an amended complaint no later than February 13, 2025. If Mr. Powell chooses to file an amended complaint, he must allege ultimate facts supporting each cause of action and must also allege ultimate facts showing that he complied with the claims filing requirements of the Government Code or that he is excused from doing so. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
